mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower the risk-free discount rate applied to equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no statistical correlation with FEDFUNDS changes over a 5-year rolling window, or Rank IC falls below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.4 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that trigger immediate equity risk-off positioning, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day forward returns remain positive in over 55% of instances where UNRATE rises > 0.4pp, or the signal loses predictive power during expansionary phases.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates broad-based consumer demand strength that supports corporate earnings growth, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive 60-day returns when RSAFS and HOUST diverge, or the interaction term coefficient becomes statistically insignificant in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in the index of industrial production below its 12-month moving average indicates supply-side bottlenecks that compress profit margins, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: INDPRO contractions fail to predict negative 20-day returns during late-cycle expansions, or the signal reverses direction during early-cycle recoveries.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate level exceeding 5.0% imposes severe financing constraints on highly leveraged firms, triggering immediate equity deleveraging and negative S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day returns show no significant negative skew when FEDFUNDS exceeds 5.0%, or the threshold effect disappears after controlling for inflation expectations.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with a falling unemployment rate creates a tight labor market that forces wage inflation, eroding corporate margins and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The combined PAYEMS and UNRATE signal fails to predict negative 60-day returns, or the interaction effect is dominated by monetary policy responses in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: A quarterly decline in real GDP growth below 1.5% signals economic stagnation that reduces consumer spending expectations, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: GDPC1 declines below 1.5% fail to predict negative 20-day returns during post-shock recoveries, or the signal becomes positive during recessionary periods.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly drop in industrial production exceeding 0.5% indicates acute supply chain disruptions that immediately reduce corporate output expectations, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day returns remain positive in over 60% of instances where INDPRO drops > 0.5%, or the signal loses predictive accuracy during periods of high inventory drawdowns.
